keyEvents.event: Start of the Century of Humiliation (Opium Wars)
keyEvents.impact: Deep national narrative of victimhood by foreign powers; obsession with sovereignty and territorial integrity

keyEvents.event: Communist revolution and founding of the PRC
keyEvents.impact: Party legitimacy rooted in national rejuvenation and ending foreign domination
Strategic self-reliance; distrust of formal alliances even with ideological partners
Regime survival prioritized above all; social stability treated as existential
Export-led boom made China the world's factory and deeply integrated into global trade
Centralization of power, assertive 'wolf warrior' diplomacy, and the China Dream of rejuvenation by 2049

keyEvents.event: US-China trade and technology war begins
keyEvents.impact: Drive for technological self-sufficiency in chips, AI, and critical supply chains

history.narrative: A rising superpower convinced that its return to centrality is the correction of a historical aberration. Plays a patient long game, preferring economic leverage and gray-zone coercion to open war, but regards Taiwan and territorial integrity as non-negotiable core interests.

redLines.description: Formal Taiwanese independence or foreign military presence on Taiwan
Challenges to sovereignty claims in the South China Sea and over Tibet/Xinjiang
Foreign-backed attempts to undermine Communist Party rule
Blockade of energy imports or strategic technology embargo

goals.description: Achieve reunification with Taiwan, by force if necessary
Achieve technological self-sufficiency in semiconductors, AI, and aerospace
Displace the United States as the dominant power in the Asia-Pacific
Break US containment via the first island chain and alliance network
Expand Belt and Road influence and de-dollarize trade with the Global South
